title: Mobile Session Timeout and Re-Authentication

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, OAuth2/OIDC, local encrypted SQLite, device secure enclave

description: Ensure inactive user sessions are terminated and protected by re-authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: User session must auto-lock after 120 seconds of inactivity (no touch/biometric interaction).
After auto-lock, access to balances or payments must require biometric re-authentication within 2 seconds on supported devices.
Session token must expire within 900 seconds after last authenticated request and must not be refreshable after expiry.

title: Account Overview API Latency Under Mobile Network

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, CDN for static assets, local encrypted SQLite cache

description: Maintain responsive account overview loading in typical mobile conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: P95 end-to-end time to load Account Overview (tap to fully rendered) must be <= 1.8 seconds on 4G with 80–120 ms RTT.
API gateway P95 latency for /accounts/summary must be <= 250 ms at 500 requests/second.
Local cache read for last-known balances must complete within 40 ms on iPhone models with 4 GB RAM or more.

title: Offline Mode Data Freshness and Recovery

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, local encrypted SQLite with write-ahead logging, background sync

description: Provide predictable behavior when connectivity drops and ensure safe synchronization.
acceptanceCriteria: When offline, app must display a data freshness indicator if cached data is older than 300 seconds.
After connectivity resumes, background sync must complete within 20 seconds for up to 200 cached transactions.
If sync fails, app must retry with exponential backoff starting at 2 seconds and must not exceed 5 retries in 10 minutes.

title: Accessibility Contrast and Dynamic Type

context.architecture: Native iOS UI, design system components, local encrypted SQLite

standard: WCAG 2.2 AA / Apple Human Interface Guidelines
description: Ensure readable UI under accessibility settings and varied lighting conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: Text-to-background contrast ratio must be >= 4.5:1 for normal text and >= 3.0:1 for large text across all primary screens.
With iOS Dynamic Type set to accessibility size (>= 200%), key actions (Pay, Transfer, Confirm) must remain visible without horizontal scrolling on screens >= 375 px width.
VoiceOver focus order must allow completing a payment in <= 12 focus steps from the first field to Confirm.

title: Input Validation Limits for Payments

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, server-side validation, client-side guards, encrypted local storage

description: Prevent injection, overflow, and malformed input for payment initiation.
acceptanceCriteria: Payee name field must accept 1–70 characters; inputs > 70 must be rejected with a validation message within 200 ms.
Reference message must accept 0–140 characters; any control characters (ASCII < 32) must be rejected 100% of the time.
IBAN input must validate length 15–34 characters and checksum; invalid IBAN must block submission with response <= 250 ms.

title: Biometric Login Time and Resource Use

context.architecture: Local secure enclave keychain, OAuth2 token exchange, encrypted SQLite

description: Optimize sign-in performance while minimizing device resource consumption.
acceptanceCriteria: Biometric authentication prompt to successful unlock must complete in <= 1.5 seconds P95 on supported devices.
Post-unlock token refresh network call must complete in <= 900 ms P95 on 5G with 40–80 ms RTT.
CPU usage attributable to the app must not exceed 18% for more than 4 consecutive seconds during login.

title: Crash-Free Sessions and Safe State Restoration

context.architecture: Native iOS app, microservices backend, encrypted local storage, structured logging

description: Maintain stability and restore user state safely after unexpected termination.
acceptanceCriteria: Crash-free session rate must be >= 99.7% measured over a rolling 30-day period.
After an app crash, relaunch must restore to the last safe screen within 2.5 seconds P95 (excluding authentication).
Any in-progress payment draft must be recoverable with <= 1 lost field value per 50 crashes (<= 2%).

title: Transport Security and Certificate Pinning

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC over TLS, API gateway, certificate pinning in client

description: Protect data in transit and reduce risk of MITM attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: All API calls must use TLS 1.2+; any TLS version < 1.2 must be rejected 100% of the time.
Certificate pin validation must complete within 80 ms and must block requests when pin mismatch occurs.
Handshake failure rate due to pinning must be < 0.2% per day (excluding environments flagged as test).

title: Error Messaging and Recovery for Transfers

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, client-side state machine, encrypted local cache

description: Provide actionable and timely error feedback for transfer failures.
acceptanceCriteria: User-visible error message must appear within 300 ms after server error response is received.
For network timeouts > 10 seconds, app must offer retry and must preserve 100% of entered transfer data.
Retry action must re-submit within 1.0 second and must not trigger duplicate transfer creation in > 99.99% of retries.

title: Concurrent Login and Token Refresh Load

context.architecture: Microservices, gRPC, centralized auth service, rate limiting

description: Maintain service performance during peak concurrent authentication events.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth service must support 20,000 concurrent token refresh requests with P95 latency <= 400 ms.
API gateway must maintain error rate <= 0.5% at 3,000 requests/second sustained for 15 minutes.
Client-side retry for 429 responses must wait at least 2 seconds and not exceed 3 retries within 60 seconds.

title: Role-Based Access Control for Warehouse Operations

context.architecture: Web SPA + REST APIs, RBAC service, PostgreSQL, Redis cache, audit logging
context.platform: Web browsers (Chrome/Edge), warehouse staff on wired/Wi-Fi

description: Ensure only authorized roles can perform inventory adjustments and shipping actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthorized requests to protected endpoints must return HTTP 403 in <= 150 ms P95.
RBAC policy evaluation must complete within 25 ms P95 for 95% of requests at 800 requests/second.
Privilege changes must propagate to all active sessions within 60 seconds (revoked role must block actions thereafter).

title: Pick-List Screen Load Time Under High Data Volume

context.architecture: Web SPA, REST APIs, pagination, server-side filtering, Redis cache
context.platform: Web browsers (Chrome/Edge), warehouse staff on Wi-Fi

description: Maintain fast rendering for pick-list operations with large datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: Pick-list page must become interactive within 2.2 seconds P95 on a 10 Mbps connection with 50 ms RTT.
API P95 latency for /picklists?status=OPEN must be <= 300 ms for pages of 50 items.
Client memory usage must not exceed 450 MB after navigating 20 pick-lists consecutively without full page refresh.

title: Inventory Update Consistency and Retry Behavior

context.architecture: Web SPA, REST APIs, PostgreSQL transactions, message queue for async updates

description: Ensure inventory updates are durable and consistent under intermittent connectivity.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory adjustment API must be idempotent using a client-generated requestId and must prevent duplicates with >= 99.999% effectiveness.
On transient 5xx errors, client must retry with backoff (2s, 4s, 8s) and stop after 3 retries within 20 seconds.
Successful adjustment must be visible in stock-on-hand queries within 5 seconds P95.

title: Barcode Input Usability and Error Prevention

context.architecture: Web SPA, REST APIs, barcode scanner as keyboard input, validation layer
context.platform: Web browsers on rugged terminals, warehouse staff

description: Reduce user errors and speed up scanning workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Barcode field must accept 8–30 characters; input outside range must be rejected within 150 ms with a clear inline message.
After a valid scan, focus must advance to the next required field within 120 ms.
For 10 consecutive scans, the system must allow completing each scan-to-confirm cycle in <= 2.0 seconds P95.

title: Session Management and Idle Timeout for Shared Workstations

context.architecture: Web SPA, SSO, server-side sessions, Redis session store
context.platform: Web browsers on shared warehouse workstations

description: Protect accounts in shared environments through strict session controls.
acceptanceCriteria: User must be logged out after 300 seconds of inactivity (no keyboard or mouse input).
After logout, navigating back must not display sensitive content; cached pages must revalidate within 1 second.
Maximum session lifetime must be 8 hours (28,800 seconds) regardless of activity.

title: Bulk Import Performance for SKU Master Data

context.architecture: Web SPA, REST upload service, background job queue, PostgreSQL

description: Enable efficient bulk upload of product master data.
acceptanceCriteria: System must process a CSV import of 50,000 SKUs (<= 25 MB file size) within 180 seconds P95.
During import, UI must show progress updates at least every 2 seconds.
Import job queue must sustain 10 concurrent imports with average throughput >= 250 SKUs/second per job.

title: Disaster Recovery RTO/RPO for Warehouse Operations

context.architecture: Active-passive deployment, PostgreSQL replication, object storage backups, IaC

description: Recover warehouse operations quickly with minimal data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be <= 30 minutes for a full region outage.
Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be <= 5 minutes for transactional data (inventory moves, shipments).
Post-recovery, critical endpoints (/pick, /ship, /inventory) must pass health checks within 3 minutes of service restoration.

title: Audit Trail Completeness for Inventory Movements

context.architecture: REST APIs, append-only audit log, centralized logging, immutable storage tier

description: Maintain a tamper-evident audit trail for critical warehouse actions.
acceptanceCriteria: 100% of inventory movement actions must create an audit record within 1 second of transaction commit.
Audit record must include userId, role, timestamp (ms precision), SKU, quantity, and source/destination locations (all non-null).
Audit log retrieval for a single SKU over 30 days must return results within 2.0 seconds for up to 10,000 entries.

title: Accessibility for Keyboard-Only Operation

context.architecture: Web SPA, semantic HTML, ARIA roles, REST APIs
context.platform: Web browsers (Chrome/Edge), warehouse and back-office users

description: Support efficient operation without a mouse for speed and accessibility.
acceptanceCriteria: All core workflows (receive, pick, pack, ship) must be completable using keyboard only with <= 25 tab stops per screen.
Focus indicator must be visible with contrast ratio >= 3.0:1 against adjacent colors on all interactive elements.
Pressing Enter on primary action buttons must trigger the action within 150 ms (client-side) after validation passes.

title: Concurrent User Load for Peak Shift Operations

context.architecture: Web SPA, REST APIs, autoscaling app tier, PostgreSQL, Redis
context.platform: Web browsers, warehouse staff on Wi-Fi

description: Maintain acceptable performance during peak warehouse shifts.
acceptanceCriteria: System must support 1,200 concurrent active users with P95 API latency <= 350 ms across core endpoints.
Overall error rate must remain <= 0.8% during 60 minutes of sustained peak load.
Search endpoint (/inventory/search) must return results within 900 ms P95 for queries over 2 million SKUs with indexed fields.

title: Device-to-Cloud Authentication and Key Rotation

context.architecture: IoT devices + MQTT broker, device certificates, ingestion microservices, time-series DB
context.platform: Medical monitoring devices over Wi-Fi/LTE, clinician web console

description: Ensure strong device identity, authentication, and periodic key rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each device must authenticate using unique X.509 certificate; shared certificates must be rejected 100% of the time.
Certificate rotation must occur at least every 90 days and complete within 10 minutes per device once initiated.
Failed authentication attempts must be rate-limited to <= 5 attempts per 60 seconds per deviceId.

title: Vital Sign Ingestion Latency and Alert Timeliness

context.architecture: MQTT ingestion, stream processing, rules engine, time-series storage
context.platform: IoT devices over Wi-Fi/LTE, clinician web/mobile alerts

description: Ensure near-real-time monitoring and alerts for abnormal vitals.
acceptanceCriteria: From device publish to data persisted in time-series DB must be <= 2.0 seconds P95 at 5,000 messages/second.
Critical alert generation (threshold breach) must occur within 3.5 seconds P95 from device publish.
Clinician dashboard refresh must render latest readings within 1.5 seconds P95 for panels with up to 50 patients.

title: Data Loss Protection with RTO/RPO for Patient Streams

context.architecture: Multi-zone deployment, replicated time-series DB, durable message queue, automated failover
context.platform: IoT devices over Wi-Fi/LTE, clinician console

standard: Business Continuity Plan / Medical Service SLA
description: Ensure continuity of monitoring during failures with defined recovery objectives.
acceptanceCriteria: RTO must be <= 10 minutes for ingestion pipeline failure affecting a single region.
RPO must be <= 30 seconds for incoming telemetry (no more than 30 seconds of data loss per patient stream).
After failover, backlog drain must complete within 15 minutes for a queue backlog of 3,000,000 messages.

title: Alarm Presentation Clarity and Acknowledgement Time

context.architecture: Rules engine, alerting service, clinician web console, audit logging
context.platform: Clinician desktop web and tablet web

description: Reduce cognitive load and ensure fast acknowledgement of critical alarms.
acceptanceCriteria: Critical alarms must be visually distinguishable with contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 between alarm text and background.
Acknowledge action must complete (UI confirmation shown) within 500 ms after server success response.
From alarm appearance, the UI must allow acknowledgement in <= 3 clicks/taps and <= 8 seconds median for trained users in usability testing (n>=10).

title: PHI Data Encryption at Rest and Access Logging

context.architecture: Time-series DB + relational metadata store, KMS-managed encryption, centralized logging

description: Protect patient health information (PHI) and ensure traceable access.
acceptanceCriteria: All PHI stored in databases and object storage must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 (100% coverage).
Decryption key access must be logged with timestamp precision to milliseconds and retained for >= 365 days.
Audit log write must occur within 1 second P95 of PHI read access for 99.9% of requests.

title: Battery and Bandwidth Constraints for Device Telemetry

context.architecture: IoT firmware, MQTT publish, edge buffering, adaptive sampling
context.platform: Battery-powered medical monitoring devices over Wi-Fi/LTE

description: Limit device resource usage while maintaining clinically useful telemetry.
acceptanceCriteria: Average telemetry bandwidth must not exceed 60 KB/s per device over any 5-minute window at normal sampling mode.
Device CPU utilization attributable to telemetry must remain <= 20% for 95% of the time during continuous monitoring.
On-device buffer must store at least 10 minutes of data (>= 600 seconds) without data loss when disconnected.

title: Store-and-Forward Reliability During Network Outages

context.architecture: Edge buffering, MQTT reconnect, message sequencing, deduplication at ingestion

description: Ensure data integrity and continuity during intermittent connectivity.
acceptanceCriteria: Device must retry connection with backoff starting at 5 seconds and not exceeding 60 seconds between attempts.
Upon reconnection, device must transmit buffered data in order within 120 seconds for a 10-minute buffer at normal sampling.
Ingestion service must detect and drop duplicates with >= 99.99% accuracy based on messageId and timestamp.

title: Dashboard Accessibility and Readability for Clinicians

context.architecture: Clinician web console, responsive UI, role-based views

description: Ensure clinicians can quickly interpret patient status with accessible UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Key patient status indicators must be readable at 200% zoom without horizontal scrolling on a 1024 px wide viewport.
Charts must provide text alternatives; screen reader must announce latest value within 2 seconds of focus on the widget.
Sorting patient list by severity must complete within 800 ms for up to 2,000 patients.

title: Input Validation and Safe Ranges for Device Configuration

context.architecture: Clinician console -> configuration service -> device command channel, validation layer

description: Prevent unsafe or malformed configuration updates to devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Heart rate alert threshold must accept values 30–220 bpm; values outside range must be rejected with HTTP 400 within 200 ms P95.
Sampling interval must accept 1–60 seconds; values < 1 or > 60 must be blocked 100% of the time.
Configuration payload size must not exceed 64 KB; payloads > 64 KB must be rejected within 150 ms P95.

title: Concurrent Device Load and Broker Throughput

context.architecture: MQTT broker cluster, autoscaling ingestion services, time-series DB
context.platform: IoT devices over Wi-Fi/LTE, clinician console

description: Sustain peak connected devices and message throughput without degraded service.
acceptanceCriteria: Broker cluster must support 50,000 concurrent device connections with connection success rate >= 99.5% over 30 minutes.
End-to-end ingestion pipeline must sustain 20,000 messages/second with P95 publish-to-persist latency <= 2.5 seconds.
Packet loss (application-level, unacknowledged publishes) must remain <= 0.3% at peak load.
